mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative in >55% of months following a rate cut, or rolling Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 6.5% signals severe labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending and corporate earnings, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in >60% of instances when UNRATE crosses 6.5%, or t-statistic of return difference is insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real personal income and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and construction demand expansion, driving positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 20-day returns with a statistical significance level of p < 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during periods of low inflation reflects strong real economic output without pricing pressure, generating positive short-term S&P 500 momentum.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during low CPI periods produce negative 5-day forward returns in >50% of cases, or the signal loses predictive power when CPIAUCSL > 2.5% YoY.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate wage income and corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC for PAYEMS growth against 60-day forward returns falls below 0.01, or returns are negative in >55% of months with payroll growth.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy and margin compression, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in >65% of months following CPIAUCSL MoM spikes above 0.4%, or the signal shows no statistical edge in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in real GDP and real personal income confirms broad-based economic strength that supports corporate earnings growth, driving positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined GDPC1 and RSAFS growth signal yields negative 60-day returns in >50% of quarters, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle signals supply-side contraction that outweighs rate hike benefits, causing negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods produce positive 5-day returns in >60% of instances, or the regime-specific predictive accuracy drops below random chance.
